Title: Directed Biosynthesis of Mitragynine Stereoisomers
Journal: Journal of the American Chemical Society

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid / corynanthe-type alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Central scaffold formation of mitragynine/speciogynine branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for C-20 stereochemical outcome
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of mitragynine and speciogynine and determines stereochemical branch preference
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reductively converts strictosidine aglycone to dihydrocorynantheine stereoisomers, producing mainly (20S)-dihydrocorynantheine and also (20R)-dihydrocorynantheine under assay conditions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Analgesic/opioid-related and smooth muscle-related bioactivities are associated with these alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of corynantheidine precursors and biases production toward the mitragynine precursor with 20S stereochemistry
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsDCS1 was identified from Mitragyna speciosa as a reductase acting on strictosidine aglycone. 2. In vitro and transient expression assays showed that it generates both stereoisomeric products but favors the 20S branch leading to mitragynine. 3. Mutagenesis of active-site residues shifted stereoselectivity, demonstrating that MsDCS1 controls C-20 stereochemistry relevant to kratom alkaloid profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome mining; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme assay with CrSGD and NADPH; LC-MS/HRMS/MS comparison with standards; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Mitragyna speciosa; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in leaves/stems relative to early strictosidine aglycone pathway genes that were root-enriched; identified from kratom transcriptome coexpression analyses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CrSGD; MsEnolMT; CrSTR (in reconstructed pathway context)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (Mitragyna speciosa transcriptome; expression shown as FPKM); targeted metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf, stem, bark, and root tissue comparisons; transient precursor feeding with tryptamine, secologanin, and methoxy/fluoro-tryptamine analogues
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Engineering stereoselectivity may alter the broader kratom alkaloid profile
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for metabolic engineering or genome editing to increase alkaloids with 20S stereochemistry and improve mitragynine production; active-site residues provide rational engineering handles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MsDCS1 product ratios varied among in vitro assays, indicating assay-condition sensitivity of stereochemical outcome; septuple mutant nearly abolished 20S product formation

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid / corynanthe-type alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Central scaffold formation of mitragynine/speciogynine branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch enzyme but not shown as the unique 20S determinant
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor pool for corynanthe-type alkaloids, favoring the 20R branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with smooth muscle relaxant activity and distinct pharmacology from mitragynine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports production of the 20R branch precursor leading to speciogynine-like alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsDCS2 was identified as a kratom reductase candidate homologous to CpDCS. 2. Enzyme assays showed it produces the 20R dihydrocorynantheine product. 3. The study proposes that reducing or mutating MsDCS2 could shift kratom alkaloid profiles toward 20S-configured products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome mining; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme assay with CrSGD and NADPH; LC-MS/HRMS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified from kratom transcriptome among homologues coexpressed with strictosidine-related genes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (Mitragyna speciosa transcriptome; FPKM); targeted metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays with strictosidine-derived aglycone
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppressing MsDCS2 to favor mitragynine may reduce 20R alkaloids and alter natural alkaloid balance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate knockout/mutation target to enrich 20S-configured alkaloids in kratom
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mining of the kratom genome suggested MsDCS1 is the only homologue carrying the residue pattern linked to strong 20S production

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Monoterpene indole alkaloid / corynanthe-type alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate methylated intermediate toward mitragynine and speciogynine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates the enol intermediate/dihydrocorynantheine-derived substrate at C-16 to form corynantheidine stereoisomers
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: (20S)- and (20R)-dihydrocorynantheine-derived intermediate; SAM as methyl donor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive kratom alkaloids with distinct pharmacology
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of corynantheidine intermediates required for biosynthesis of mitragynine and speciogynine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsEnolMT was the only coexpressed methyltransferase candidate showing activity in pathway assays. 2. Coincubation with MsDCS1, MsDCS2, or CpDCS generated methylated corynantheidine products. 3. This enzyme completes the demonstrated scaffold-forming sequence toward major kratom corynanthe alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression-guided candidate selection; purification of candidates; in vitro combinatorial enzyme assays with CrSGD, DCS enzymes, NADPH, and SAM; LC-MS/HRMS/MS; comparison to authentic standard; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed strongly with MsDCS1 in the kratom transcriptome (reported Pearson r = 0.96)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MsDCS1; MsDCS2; CpDCS; CrSGD; CrSTR (in reconstructed pathway context)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (Mitragyna speciosa transcriptome; coexpression and FPKM); targeted metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient precursor feeding with tryptamine, secologanin, 4-methoxytryptamine, and fluorinated tryptamines
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Engineering may channel flux into methylated corynanthe intermediates and alter downstream alkaloid spectrum
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway component for heterologous production platforms and directed biosynthesis of mitragynine, speciogynine, and analogues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Activity was observed in all tested DCS-coupled assays, supporting broad acceptance of stereoisomeric DCS products
